label: Taurine
target: 250–500 mg per 4 kg cat per day. Mandatory in cooked diets — cooking destroys ~20%. Required in raw diets if hearts aren't a major component.

sources.label: Pure taurine powder
sources.dose: Sprinkle 250–500 mg per day. Water-soluble, excess is excreted.

sources.label: Heart cuts (chicken, beef, turkey)
sources.dose: Hearts are the richest natural source. Aim for ≥ 30% of muscle meat from hearts in raw diets.

sources.label: Dark poultry meat
sources.dose: Higher taurine than breast meat; rotate frequently.

label: Calcium
target: ~900–1100 mg per day for a 4 kg cat fed boneless meat. Meat alone is Ca:P ≈ 1:20 — calcium supplementation is mandatory in any boneless homemade diet.

sources.label: Ground eggshell
sources.dose: ¼ tsp per 1 lb meat (1 large eggshell ≈ 2000 mg Ca)

sources.label: Bone meal (food grade)
sources.dose: ¼ tsp per 1 lb meat — verify lead-free source

sources.label: MCHA (Microcrystalline Hydroxyapatite)
sources.dose: Per label — provides Ca + P together; check Vit D content

label: Vitamin E
target: 10–30 IU per day. Mandatory whenever fish oil or high-PUFA meats are fed — prevents steatitis (yellow fat disease).

sources.label: Vitamin E supplement
sources.dose: 10–30 IU/day per 4 kg cat — natural d-alpha tocopherol preferred

label: B-complex (incl. thiamine, B6, niacin)
target: Cats need ~4× the niacin dogs do. Water-soluble B vitamins are lost in cooking liquid — supplement is mandatory in cooked diets.

sources.label: Feline B-complex supplement
sources.dose: Per product label — choose a balanced complex, not isolated B vitamins

sources.label: Nutritional yeast (fortified)
sources.dose: ½ tsp/day for a 4 kg cat — natural B-vitamin source

label: Iodine
target: 0.1–0.2 mg per day. Required for thyroid function. Muscle meat alone is iodine-poor.

sources.label: Kelp powder
sources.dose: Pinch (≈ 100 mg of kelp powder ≈ 0.1 mg iodine) — verify iodine content per product

sources.label: Iodized salt (tiny pinch)
sources.dose: 1/16 tsp per day max — avoid sodium overload

label: Omega-3 (EPA + DHA)
target: 75–150 mg combined EPA + DHA per day for a 4 kg cat. Pair with vitamin E to prevent oxidative damage.

sources.label: Fish oil (anchovy, sardine)
sources.dose: Per concentration — refrigerate, replace every 2 months once opened

sources.label: Sardines (whole, in water)
sources.dose: ½ sardine 2–3× weekly for a 4 kg cat

label: Pre-formed vitamin A
target: Cats cannot convert beta-carotene — pre-formed retinol only. Liver provides it but has a strict ceiling.

sources.label: Liver (chicken, beef)
sources.dose: Cap at 5–10 g/day for a 4 kg cat — never more (vitamin A toxicity)

label: Arachidonic acid
target: Cats lack delta-6-desaturase — they cannot synthesize AA from linoleic acid. Must come from animal fat.

sources.label: Animal fat (chicken skin, beef tallow)
sources.dose: Included naturally if feeding skin-on poultry or fatty cuts

sources.label: Egg yolk
sources.dose: 1–2 yolks per week for a 4 kg cat — cooked recommended
